title: 2019 Coolest Tool Award
description: The first ever Coolest Tool Award ceremony took place during Wikimania 2019, as part of the Wikimania program. We have recognized some of the greatest tools in a variety of categories: Experience, Tiny, Impact, Reusable, Editor, Developer, Mobile, Newcomer, Outreach, Eggbeater (= tools that have been around for a long time). Learn more at https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2019

tools.name: toolforge-locator-tool
tools.title: locator-tool
tools.description:
Tool to add {{Location}} or {{Object location}} to images on Wikimedia Commons

Fetch images on Wikimedia Commons via category or user contributions, shows markers for {{Location}} or {{Object location}} on map, allows to change {{Location}} or {{Object location}} and save the description page.

tools.name: commons-gadget-hotcat
tools.title: HotCat
tools.description: Gadget that augments the category bar with quick links to remove, change, or add categories.

tools.name: iabot
tools.title: IABot Management Interface
tools.description: InternetArchiveBot is an advanced bot that identifies and replaces broken external links. Use this interface to run the bot on one or more pages, or to configure its operation on a wiki.

tools.name: pageviews
tools.title: Pageviews Analysis
tools.description: Analysis of pageviews across multiple pages

tools.name: quickstatements
tools.title: QuickStatements
tools.description: Tool to edit Wikidata items, based on a simple set of text commands. The tool can add and remove statements, labels, descriptions and aliases; as well as add statements with optional qualifiers and sources.

tools.name: wmcloud-codesearch
tools.title: MediaWiki Codesearch
tools.description: MediaWiki Codesearch is a fully free software tool that lets you easily search through all of MediaWiki core, extensions, and skins that are hosted on Gerrit. You can limit your search to specific repositories, or types of repositories too. Regular expressions are supported in both the search string, and when filtering by path.

tools.name: android-commons-app
tools.title: Commons Mobile App
tools.description: The Commons Mobile App is a community-maintained app that makes it easy to upload your pictures to Wikimedia Commons. The app can be installed on any Android device and uses your Commons user account to upload photos.

tools.name: programs-and-events-dashboard
tools.title: Programs and Events Dashboard
tools.description: A comprehensive app to organize and track Edit-a-thons, Education Program classes, and other programs. Features include training modules, automatically updated editing stats, article quality visualizations, and more.

tools.name: enwiki-gadget-twinkle
tools.title: Twinkle
tools.description: Twinkle is a popular JavaScript Wikipedia gadget that allows autoconfirmed users to have many extra options to assist them in common Wikipedia maintenance tasks and to help them deal with acts of vandalism or unconstructive edits. It provides users with three types of rollback functions and includes a full library of speedy deletion functions, user warnings and welcomes, maintenance tags, semi-automatic reporting of vandals, and more. In addition, it gives administrators more tools to help them carry out their duties.
